[[de:Wepet-renpet]] [[de:Wepet-renpet]] [[de:Wepet-renpet]] {| class="Box center" style="width:350px; border-collapse:collapse;" |- ! colspan="3"|Opener of the Year |- | Name: |class="hiero" style="width:155px;"|F15 |rowspan="2" style="width:85px;"|Wepet-renpet
''Wpt-rnp.t'' |- |In the ceiling
image in the tomb
of Senenmut
(schematic, mirrored) |class="hiero"|
F15
[[File:SenenmutRad.svg|thumb]] |- ! colspan="3"|Emergence of the goddess [[:en:Sopdet|Sopdet]] |- | Name: |class="hiero"|pr:D21-X1:D54 M44-t:N14 |Peret-Sopdet
''Prt-Spd.t''According to the transcription from the Ebers calendar in {{Literatur |Autor=Kurt Sethe |Hrsg=Georg Steindorff |Titel=Urkunden der 18. Dynastie |Sammelwerk=Urkunden des ägyptischen Altertums |Band=Band 1: ''Historisch biographische Urkunden'' |Verlag=Hinrichs’sche Buchhandlung |Ort=Leipzig |Datum=1906-01-01 |Seiten=44 |DNB=560991142}} |- ! colspan="3"|The Month in the Seasons |- | Number and
Season |class="hiero"|{{Hiero/SeasonDate|Season=3|Month=4}} |rowspan="2"|4th month
in [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]]
''4-nw-Šmw'' |- |As above, in
the Ramesseum
(mirrored) |class="hiero"|{{Hiero/SeasonDate|Season=3|Month=4|Ramses=yes}} |- |colspan="3"|Note:
Wikipedia is currently set to display [[:en:Egyptian hieroglyphs|Egyptian hieroglyphs]] in left-to-right writing direction. In the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]Calendar depiction in the astronomical ceiling image in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]] († ~1460 BCE), [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]], {{ill|TT353|de|TT353|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/TT353|qq=|WD=Q666212}}. A high-resolution scan is available online at the [https://images.metmuseum.org/CRDImages/eg/original/48.105.52_EGDP012289.jpg Metropolitan Museum of Art] in New York. (Accessed November 14, 2021) and in the Ramesseum{{Literatur |Hrsg=C. R. Lepsius |Titel=Denkmäler des neuen Reichs |Sammelwerk=Denkmäler aus Ägypten und Äthiopien nach den Zeichnungen der von seiner Majestät dem Könige von Preußen Friedrich Wilhelm IV nach diesen Ländern gesendeten und in den Jahren 1842-1845 ausgeführten wissenschaftlichen Expedition |Band=Band VI, III. Abteilung |Verlag=Nicolaische Buchhandlung |Ort=Berlin |Datum=1849 |Seiten=171 |Online=https://edoc3.bibliothek.uni-halle.de/lepsius/tafelwa3.html |Umfang=Blatt XCI-CLXXII}}, this orientation is reversed.
|} '''Wepet-renpet''', in German "Opener of the Year," referred in ancient Egypt to the {{ill|heliacal rising|de|heliakisch|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/heliacal rising|qq=|WD=Q368373}} of the star [[:en:Sirius|Sirius]]. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|WD=Q1944246}}, this event signified that the following first sunrise after the new moon marked the beginning of the new month, the onset of [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]], and thus the new year.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (IL) 1950, p. 37, §191. The same term was later also used to name a month. In the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]], this was the last month in the {{ill|Egyptian administrative calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian administrative calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil calendar|qq=|WD=Q108862211}}.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (IL) 1950, p. 45, §230/Table 7. The Egyptian name for Sirius was Sopdet, which was Hellenized to Σῶθις [ˈsoːtis].{{Literatur |Autor=Franz Passow |Titel=Handwörterbuch der griechischen Sprache |TitelErg=neu bearbeitet und zeitgemäß umgestaltet |Band=2 |Nummer=2 |Auflage=5. |Verlag=Fr. Chr. Wilh. Vogel |Ort=Leipzig |Datum=1857-01-01 |DNB=990890414}} The Sothis rising ''Peret Sopdet'' was fixed as a festival in the administrative calendar. == Background == [[File:Senenmut-Grab.JPG|thumb|260px|Calendar of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]]] [[:en:Amenhotep I|Amenhotep I]] dated the Sothis appearance in the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=|WD=Q1279717}} to the 9th day of the third [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] month.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (IL) 1950, p. 37, §191. This corresponds to the first half of the 11th month. In Richard A. Parker's ''Calendars of Ancient Egypt,'' it is argued that the Ebers calendar must have been the administrative calendar, as the month reference is tied to the seasons. However, the Sothis rising, like other "festivals of cycles" celebrated only once a year, was celebrated according to the wandering year of the administrative calendar.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' In: ''Abhandlungen der geistes- und sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' No. 10. Mainz 1950, p. (46). While the Ebers calendar seemingly places the month of Wepet-renpet at the forefront, it is listed in the month overviews in the {{ill|Tomb TT353|de|TT353|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb TT353|qq=|WD=Q666212}} of Senenmut, as well as in the corresponding festival calendars of [[:en:Hatshepsut|Hatshepsut]] and [[:en:Thutmose III|Thutmose III]], as the twelfth and final month. == See Also == * {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|WD=Q1944246}} * {{ill|Egyptian administrative calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian administrative calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil calendar|qq=|WD=Q108862211}} == References == * [[:en:Rolf Krauss|Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard A. Parker]]: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26). The University of Chicago Press, Chicago (IL) 1950. * {{Literatur |Autor=[[Siegfried Schott]] |Hrsg=Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur |Titel=Altägyptische Festdaten. |Sammelwerk=Abhandlungen der geistes- und sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse |Nummer=10 |Ort=Mainz |Datum=1950-10-27 |DNB=454460953}} == References == {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Wepet-renpet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Wepet-renpet]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] {{wikidata link|Q2559877}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

In the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]Calendar depiction in the astronomical ceiling image in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]] († ~1460 BCE), [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes (Egypt)]], {{ill|TT353|de|TT353|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/TT353|qq=|WD=Q666212}}. A high-resolution scan is available online at the [https://images.metmuseum.org/CRDImages/eg/original/48.105.52_EGDP012289.jpg Metropolitan Museum of Art] in New York. (Accessed November 14, 2021) and in the Ramesseum{{Literatur |Hrsg=C. R. Lepsius |Titel=Denkmäler des neuen Reichs |Sammelwerk=Denkmäler aus Ägypten und Äthiopien nach den Zeichnungen der von seiner Majestät dem Könige von Preußen Friedrich Wilhelm IV nach diesen Ländern gesendeten und in den Jahren 1842-1845 ausgeführten wissenschaftlichen Expedition |Band=Band VI, III. Abteilung |Verlag=Nicolaische Buchhandlung |Ort=Berlin |Datum=1849 |Seiten=171 |Online=https://edoc3.bibliothek.uni-halle.de/lepsius/tafelwa3.html |Umfang=Blatt XCI-CLXXII}}, this orientation is reversed.
|} '''Wepet-renpet''', in German "Opener of the Year," referred in ancient Egypt to the {{ill|heliacal rising|de|heliakisch|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/heliacal rising|qq=|WD=Q368373}} of the star [[:en:Sirius|Sirius]]. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|WD=Q1944246}}, this event signified that the following first sunrise after the new moon marked the beginning of the new month, the onset of [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]], and thus the new year.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (IL) 1950, p. 37, §191. The same term was later also used to name a month. In the [[:en:New Kingdom of Egypt|New Kingdom]], this was the last month in the {{ill|Egyptian administrative calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian administrative calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil calendar|qq=|WD=Q108862211}}.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (IL) 1950, p. 45, §230/Table 7. The Egyptian name for Sirius was Sopdet, which was Hellenized to Σῶθις [ˈsoːtis].{{Literatur |Autor=Franz Passow |Titel=Handwörterbuch der griechischen Sprache |TitelErg=neu bearbeitet und zeitgemäß umgestaltet |Band=2 |Nummer=2 |Auflage=5. |Verlag=Fr. Chr. Wilh. Vogel |Ort=Leipzig |Datum=1857-01-01 |DNB=990890414}} The Sothis rising ''Peret Sopdet'' was fixed as a festival in the administrative calendar. == Background == [[File:Senenmut-Grab.JPG|thumb|260px|Calendar of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]]] [[:en:Amenhotep I|Amenhotep I]] dated the Sothis appearance in the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=|WD=Q1279717}} to the 9th day of the third [[:en:Season of the Harvest|Schemu]] month.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (IL) 1950, p. 37, §191. This corresponds to the first half of the 11th month. In Richard A. Parker's ''Calendars of Ancient Egypt,'' it is argued that the Ebers calendar must have been the administrative calendar, as the month reference is tied to the seasons. However, the Sothis rising, like other "festivals of cycles" celebrated only once a year, was celebrated according to the wandering year of the administrative calendar.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' In: ''Abhandlungen der geistes- und sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' No. 10. Mainz 1950, p. (46). While the Ebers calendar seemingly places the month of Wepet-renpet at the forefront, it is listed in the month overviews in the {{ill|Tomb TT353|de|TT353|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb TT353|qq=|WD=Q666212}} of Senenmut, as well as in the corresponding festival calendars of [[:en:Hatshepsut|Hatshepsut]] and [[:en:Thutmose III|Thutmose III]], as the twelfth and final month. == See Also == * {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|WD=Q1944246}} * {{ill|Egyptian administrative calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian administrative calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil calendar|qq=|WD=Q108862211}} == References == * [[:en:Rolf Krauss|Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard A. Parker]]: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26). The University of Chicago Press, Chicago (IL) 1950. * {{Literatur |Autor=[[Siegfried Schott]] |Hrsg=Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur |Titel=Altägyptische Festdaten. |Sammelwerk=Abhandlungen der geistes- und sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse |Nummer=10 |Ort=Mainz |Datum=1950-10-27 |DNB=454460953}} == References == {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Wepet-renpet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Wepet-renpet]] {{wikidata link|Q2559877}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|WD=Q1944246}}, this event signified that the following first sunrise after the new moon marked the beginning of the new month, the onset of {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en|Season of the Inundation|WD=Q770568|lt=Achet}}, and thus the new year.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (IL) 1950, p. 37, §191. The same term was later also used to name a month. In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom}}, this was the last month in the {{ill|Egyptian administrative calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian administrative calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil calendar|qq=|WD=Q108862211}}.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (IL) 1950, p. 45, §230/Table 7. The Egyptian name for Sirius was Sopdet, which was Hellenized to Σῶθις [ˈsoːtis].{{Literatur |Autor=Franz Passow |Titel=Handwörterbuch der griechischen Sprache |TitelErg=neu bearbeitet und zeitgemäß umgestaltet |Band=2 |Nummer=2 |Auflage=5. |Verlag=Fr. Chr. Wilh. Vogel |Ort=Leipzig |Datum=1857-01-01 |DNB=990890414}} The Sothis rising ''Peret Sopdet'' was fixed as a festival in the administrative calendar. == Background == [[File:Senenmut-Grab.JPG|thumb|260px|Calendar of {{ill|Senenmut|en|Senenmut|WD=Q328732|lt=Senenmut}}]] {{ill|Amenhotep I|en|Amenhotep I|WD=Q157966|lt=Amenhotep I}} dated the Sothis appearance in the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=|WD=Q1279717}} to the 9th day of the third {{ill|Season of the Harvest|en|Season of the Harvest|WD=Q1248622|lt=Schemu}} month.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (IL) 1950, p. 37, §191. This corresponds to the first half of the 11th month. In Richard A. Parker's ''Calendars of Ancient Egypt,'' it is argued that the Ebers calendar must have been the administrative calendar, as the month reference is tied to the seasons. However, the Sothis rising, like other "festivals of cycles" celebrated only once a year, was celebrated according to the wandering year of the administrative calendar.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' In: ''Abhandlungen der geistes- und sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' No. 10. Mainz 1950, p. (46). While the Ebers calendar seemingly places the month of Wepet-renpet at the forefront, it is listed in the month overviews in the {{ill|Tomb TT353|de|TT353|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb TT353|qq=|WD=Q666212}} of Senenmut, as well as in the corresponding festival calendars of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut}} and {{ill|Thutmose III|en|Thutmose III|WD=Q157899|lt=Thutmose III}}, as the twelfth and final month. == See Also == * {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|WD=Q1944246}} * {{ill|Egyptian administrative calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian administrative calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil calendar|qq=|WD=Q108862211}} == References == * [[:en:Rolf Krauss|Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|en|Richard Anthony Parker|WD=Q2148992|lt=Richard A. Parker}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26). The University of Chicago Press, Chicago (IL) 1950. * {{Literatur |Autor=[[Siegfried Schott]] |Hrsg=Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur |Titel=Altägyptische Festdaten. |Sammelwerk=Abhandlungen der geistes- und sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse |Nummer=10 |Ort=Mainz |Datum=1950-10-27 |DNB=454460953}} == References == {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Wepet-renpet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Wepet-renpet]] {{wikidata link|Q2559877}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

image in the tomb
of Senenmut
(schematic, mirrored) |class="hiero"|
F15
[[File:SenenmutRad.svg|thumb]] |- ! colspan="3"|Emergence of the goddess {{ill|Sopdet|en|Sopdet|WD=Q930167|lt=Sopdet}} |- | Name: |class="hiero"|pr:D21-X1:D54 M44-t:N14 |Peret-Sopdet
''Prt-Spd.t''According to the transcription from the Ebers calendar in {{Literatur |Autor=Kurt Sethe |Hrsg=Georg Steindorff |Titel=Urkunden der 18. Dynastie |Sammelwerk=Urkunden des ägyptischen Altertums |Band=Band 1: ''Historisch biographische Urkunden'' |Verlag=Hinrichs’sche Buchhandlung |Ort=Leipzig |Datum=1906-01-01 |Seiten=44 |DNB=560991142}} |- ! colspan="3"|The Month in the Seasons |- | Number and

in {{ill|Season of the Harvest|en|Season of the Harvest|WD=Q1248622|lt=Schemu}}
''4-nw-Šmw'' |- |As above, in
the Ramesseum
(mirrored) |class="hiero"|{{Hiero/SeasonDate|Season=3|Month=4|Ramses=yes}} |- |colspan="3"|Note:
Wikipedia is currently set to display {{ill|Egyptian hieroglyphs|en|Egyptian hieroglyphs|WD=Q132659|lt=Egyptian hieroglyphs}} in left-to-right writing direction. In the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|en|Senenmut|WD=Q328732|lt=Senenmut}}Calendar depiction in the astronomical ceiling image in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|en|Senenmut|WD=Q328732|lt=Senenmut}} († ~1460 BCE), {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|en|Thebes, Egypt|WD=Q101583|lt=Thebes (Egypt)}}, {{ill|TT353|de|TT353|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/TT353|qq=|WD=Q666212}}. A high-resolution scan is available online at the [https://images.metmuseum.org/CRDImages/eg/original/48.105.52_EGDP012289.jpg Metropolitan Museum of Art] in New York. (Accessed November 14, 2021) and in the Ramesseum{{Literatur |Hrsg=C. R. Lepsius |Titel=Denkmäler des neuen Reichs |Sammelwerk=Denkmäler aus Ägypten und Äthiopien nach den Zeichnungen der von seiner Majestät dem Könige von Preußen Friedrich Wilhelm IV nach diesen Ländern gesendeten und in den Jahren 1842-1845 ausgeführten wissenschaftlichen Expedition |Band=Band VI, III. Abteilung |Verlag=Nicolaische Buchhandlung |Ort=Berlin |Datum=1849 |Seiten=171 |Online=https://edoc3.bibliothek.uni-halle.de/lepsius/tafelwa3.html |Umfang=Blatt XCI-CLXXII}}, this orientation is reversed.
|} '''Wepet-renpet''', in German "Opener of the Year," referred in ancient Egypt to the {{ill|heliacal rising|de|heliakisch|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/heliacal rising|qq=|WD=Q368373}} of the star {{ill|Sirius|en|Sirius|WD=Q3409|lt=Sirius}}. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|WD=Q1944246}}, this event signified that the following first sunrise after the new moon marked the beginning of the new month, the onset of {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en|Season of the Inundation|WD=Q770568|lt=Achet}}, and thus the new year.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (IL) 1950, p. 37, §191. The same term was later also used to name a month. In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom}}, this was the last month in the {{ill|Egyptian administrative calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian administrative calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil calendar|qq=|WD=Q108862211}}.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (IL) 1950, p. 45, §230/Table 7. The Egyptian name for Sirius was Sopdet, which was Hellenized to Σῶθις [ˈsoːtis].{{Literatur |Autor=Franz Passow |Titel=Handwörterbuch der griechischen Sprache |TitelErg=neu bearbeitet und zeitgemäß umgestaltet |Band=2 |Nummer=2 |Auflage=5. |Verlag=Fr. Chr. Wilh. Vogel |Ort=Leipzig |Datum=1857-01-01 |DNB=990890414}} The Sothis rising ''Peret Sopdet'' was fixed as a festival in the administrative calendar. == Background == [[File:Senenmut-Grab.JPG|thumb|260px|Calendar of {{ill|Senenmut|en|Senenmut|WD=Q328732|lt=Senenmut}}]] {{ill|Amenhotep I|en|Amenhotep I|WD=Q157966|lt=Amenhotep I}} dated the Sothis appearance in the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=|WD=Q1279717}} to the 9th day of the third {{ill|Season of the Harvest|en|Season of the Harvest|WD=Q1248622|lt=Schemu}} month.R. Abteilung |Verlag=Nicolaische Buchhandlung |Ort=Berlin |Datum=1849 |Seiten=171 |Online=https://edoc3.bibliothek.uni-halle.de/lepsius/tafelwa3.html |Umfang=Blatt XCI-CLXXII}}, this orientation is reversed.
|} '''Wepet-renpet''', in German "Opener of the Year," referred in ancient Egypt to the {{ill|heliacal rising|de|heliakisch|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/heliacal rising|qq=|WD=Q368373|1=Heliacal rising}} of the star {{ill|Sirius|en|Sirius|WD=Q3409|lt=Sirius|1=Sirius}}. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|WD=Q1944246}}, this event signified that the following first sunrise after the new moon marked the beginning of the new month, the onset of {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en|Season of the Inundation|WD=Q770568|lt=Achet|1=Season of the Inundation}}, and thus the new year.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (IL) 1950, p. 37, §191. The same term was later also used to name a month. In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}}, this was the last month in the {{ill|Egyptian administrative calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian administrative calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil calendar|qq=|WD=Q108862211}}.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (IL) 1950, p. 45, §230/Table 7. The Egyptian name for Sirius was Sopdet, which was Hellenized to Σῶθις [ˈsoːtis].{{Literatur |Autor=Franz Passow |Titel=Handwörterbuch der griechischen Sprache |TitelErg=neu bearbeitet und zeitgemäß umgestaltet |Band=2 |Nummer=2 |Auflage=5. |Verlag=Fr. Chr. Wilh. Vogel |Ort=Leipzig |Datum=1857-01-01 |DNB=990890414}} The Sothis rising ''Peret Sopdet'' was fixed as a festival in the administrative calendar. == Background == [[File:Senenmut-Grab.JPG|thumb|260px|Calendar of {{ill|Senenmut|en|Senenmut|WD=Q328732|lt=Senenmut|1=Senenmut}}]] {{ill|Amenhotep I|en|Amenhotep I|WD=Q157966|lt=Amenhotep I|1=Amenhotep I}} dated the Sothis appearance in the {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=|WD=Q1279717|1=Ebers calendar}} to the 9th day of the third {{ill|Season of the Harvest|en|Season of the Harvest|WD=Q1248622|lt=Schemu|1=Season of the Harvest}} month.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (IL) 1950, p. 37, §191. This corresponds to the first half of the 11th month. In Richard A. Parker's ''Calendars of Ancient Egypt,'' it is argued that the Ebers calendar must have been the administrative calendar, as the month reference is tied to the seasons. However, the Sothis rising, like other "festivals of cycles" celebrated only once a year, was celebrated according to the wandering year of the administrative calendar.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' In: ''Abhandlungen der geistes- und sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse.'' No. 10. Mainz 1950, p. (46). While the Ebers calendar seemingly places the month of Wepet-renpet at the forefront, it is listed in the month overviews in the {{ill|Tomb TT353|de|TT353|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb TT353|qq=|WD=Q666212|1=Tomb TT353}} of Senenmut, as well as in the corresponding festival calendars of {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|WD=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut|1=Hatshepsut}} and {{ill|Thutmose III|en|Thutmose III|WD=Q157899|lt=Thutmose III|1=Thutmose III}}, as the twelfth and final month. == See Also == * {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|WD=Q1944246}} * {{ill|Egyptian administrative calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian administrative calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil calendar|qq=|WD=Q108862211}} == References == * [[:en:Rolf Krauss|Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|en|Richard Anthony Parker|WD=Q2148992|lt=Richard A. Parker|1=Richard Anthony Parker}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt'' (= ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26). The University of Chicago Press, Chicago (IL) 1950. * {{Literatur |Autor=[[Siegfried Schott]] |Hrsg=Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur |Titel=Altägyptische Festdaten. |Sammelwerk=Abhandlungen der geistes- und sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse |Nummer=10 |Ort=Mainz |Datum=1950-10-27 |DNB=454460953}} == References == {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Wepet-renpet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Wepet-renpet]] {{wikidata link|Q2559877}} [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages]]
